name: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) Trading Agent
description: An RL agent learns to trade by interacting with a simulated market environment. State = vector of market features. Actions = {buy, sell, hold}. Reward = risk-adjusted return (Sharpe or Calmar ratio). Agent trained via Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) or Deep Q-Networks (DQN).
core_logic: State = [RSI, MACD_hist, trend, vol, position]. Q(s,a) = expected cumulative reward from state s taking action a. Policy: argmax_a Q(s,a). Training: replay buffer + target network + PPO clipping.

sharpe_ratio.note: highly variable; depends on training quality

risk.main_risk: Overfitting to historical regime; distribution shift

pros: Learns complex patterns without feature engineering
Can incorporate transaction costs in reward
signal_buy: Agent Q(s, buy) > Q(s, sell) and Q(s, buy) > threshold
signal_sell: Agent Q(s, sell) > Q(s, buy) and Q(s, sell) > threshold
